t_ch: 我确实想拒绝掉这个「商务合作」…但无论拒绝还是点头，于情于理都应该去当面给出回应。
t_en: I'm leaning towards declining this business partnership... but whether I refuse or agree to it, it seems right to give a response in person.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练，陪我去一趟金人巷吧。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, can you head over to Aurum Alley with me?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练，陪我去一趟金人巷吧。我得当面拒绝他们。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, can you head over to Aurum Alley with me? I need to decline their offer in person.

t_ch: 现在拒绝或接受都为时过早，还是和公司见一面再下判断吧。
t_en: It's too early to decide. Let's meet with the IPC first before making a decision.

t_ch: 又见面啦，卢卡，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Good to see you again, Luka, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 托帕小姐，你好啊。
t_en: Hello, Miss Topaz.

t_ch: 虽然我们三个已经算是熟人了，但我还是郑重地通过组委会那边传达了合作意愿。
t_en: Even though we are acquainted, I had formally expressed my intention to collaborate with you through the committee.

t_ch: 战略投资部希望聘请卢卡选手成为我们的品牌代言人，并在整个产品线上进行深度合作。
t_en: The Strategic Investment Department would like to hire Luka as our brand ambassador, working closely with him across our entire product line.

t_ch: 这一方面有利于提升卢卡选手的知名度——也就是说，全银河都会看到你「铁臂卢卡」的广告招牌。
t_en: This role would boost Luka's visibility — "Luka the Strongarm" could become a name featured on billboards throughout the cosmos.

t_ch: 此后贝洛伯格作为巨星的家乡，一定能获得更多的发展机遇！
t_en: Then Belobog, as the hometown of a superstar, would certainly attract more development opportunities!

t_ch: 无论从贝洛伯格还是从公司的角度来讲，这份代言合同都是好事。
t_en: This endorsement benefits both Belobog and the IPC.

t_ch: 听起来确实不错。
t_en: It does sound promising.

t_ch: 喔，那当然不错～我一贯的原则，就是绝对要让合作伙伴拿到实惠。
t_en: Absolutely. My principle has always been to ensure our partners get real, tangible benefits.

t_ch: 公司会这么好心？
t_en: Is the IPC really that generous?

t_ch: 嗯…公司当然没有那么好心，我也不是什么慈善家。这是一桩双赢的生意，仅此而已。
t_en: Well... The IPC isn't exactly generous, and I'm certainly not running a charity. It's purely a mutually beneficial business deal, nothing more.

t_ch: 你怎么总惦记着贝洛伯格？
t_en: Why do you keep focusing on Belobog?

t_ch: 那是当然的了，毕竟这可是我经手的案子。难道你们不想为贝洛伯格做些什么吗？从这个角度来看，我们可是一边的。
t_en: Because I'm the one who worked on this case, naturally. Don't you want to contribute something to Belobog? We are on the same side in this regard.

t_ch: …容我稍微思考一下，可以吗？
t_en: ...May I take a moment to think this over, if that's okay?

t_ch: 没问题，你慢慢考虑～这次合作邀约是出于互惠互利的善意，我希望你能抛开过去对公司的印象，借这次合作结下更深的信任。
t_en: Take all the time you need~ This invitation is offered in the spirit of mutual benefit and goodwill. I hope you can set aside your previous impressions of the IPC and build deeper trust through this collaboration.

t_ch: ……很抱歉，到头来我还是得拒绝您的好意。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, but I have to decline your generous offer.

t_ch: 看来你的信任十分珍贵，你不想随随便便把它交给公司。
t_en: Your trust appears to be precious, and you're not willing to give it away to the IPC lightly.

t_ch: 不，托帕小姐，我非常信任你的善意。我们交过手，所以我很清楚你是否值得信任。
t_en: No, Miss Topaz, I deeply trust your intentions. Having clashed against you before, I can confidently say you are trustworthy.

t_ch: 只是…我来参赛的初衷是让世界看到贝洛伯格。如果与公司合作，会让我觉得自己的行为不那么纯粹。
t_en: But... my initial reason for competing was to showcase Belobog to the entire universe. Partnering with the IPC might make me doubt the purity of my actions.

t_ch: 我相信托帕小姐的善意，但…贝洛伯格是否准备好接受公司的善意，又应当如何接受公司的善意…这我真的不知道，一时也拿不定主意。
t_en: While I trust Miss Topaz's intentions, I'm... not sure if Belobog is ready to accept the IPC's offer, or how we should accept that offer... I can't make a decision right now.

t_ch: ……感谢你的坦诚，卢卡选手。如果你想通了，随时联系我～
t_en: ...Thank you for your honesty, Luka. If you change your mind, feel free to let me know anytime.

t_ch: 哦对了，听说你接下了那个名叫「铄金」的选手的挑战了？
t_en: Oh, by the way, I heard you accepted the challenge from that fighter, Shuojin?

t_ch: 你说那场「邀请赛」？为什么突然问起这个？
t_en: Are you referring to the invitational match? Why bring this up all of a sudden?

t_ch: 就当是个旁观者的提醒，当心点。那家伙可有不少盘外招。
t_en: Just a heads-up from an observer. Be careful. That guy has plenty of dirty tricks.

t_ch: 感谢你的提醒，我会注意的。{NICKNAME}教练，我们也该回休息室了。
t_en: Thanks for the heads-up. I'll keep that in mind. Coach {NICKNAME}, we should head back to the lounge now.

t_ch: 感谢你的坦诚，卢卡选手。如果你想通了，随时联系我～
t_en: Thanks for your frankness, Luka. If you change your mind, you can reach out anytime.

t_ch: 盘外招？我以前的比赛生涯里，倒是遇到过那种喜欢使盘外招的卑鄙拳击手，有个家伙专在赛前给别人的饮料里下泻药。
t_en: Dirty tricks? I've come across some fighters who like to rely on dirty tricks in my days in the ring, like one guy who always snuck laxatives into his opponents' drinks.

t_ch: 巧了嘿，我也认识这么个剑客…
t_en: Interesting. I know a blade master who does that too.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，教练你还真是什么人都认识！
t_en: Hahaha, you sure know a variety of people, coach!

t_ch: 那你可要看牢你的水壶了。
t_en: Better keep an eye on your water bottle then.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这就要靠教练你帮我严防死守啦！
t_en: Hahaha, coach, I'm counting on you to keep a close watch!

t_ch: 你们两个怎么还有心思开玩笑！卢卡选手！出大事了！
t_en: Are you two really joking right now? Luka, we've got a serious situation!

t_ch: 啊？出什么大事了？你先别着急，慢慢说……
t_en: What's going on? Take a deep breath and tell us slowly...

t_ch: 我带来了赛场外的报纸，你们看！
t_en: I've brought a newspaper from the arena. Take a look!

t_ch: 去网上看看吧，现在有一大票人都在讨论「卢卡塌房，竟是公司走狗」的事情，闹得非常大！
t_en: Check online. There's a lot of buzz about Luka being an IPC puppet. It's causing quite a stir!

t_ch: 这…先不论这里面的内容都是捏造的…我和托帕小姐见面，才是不到一个系统时之前的事吧？这怎么都登报了？
t_en: ...Apart from the fact that the content might be fabricated... I just met with Miss Topaz less than a system hour ago. How did it get published so quickly?

t_ch: 这里信息流动速度和贝城不同。
t_en: The speed of information flow here is different from Belobog.

t_ch: 唉…虽然之前就感觉到了，这还是我第一次实际体会这信息流动速度有多可怕……
t_en: Sigh... Even though I already kinda guessed it, this is the first time I've truly realized how terrifyingly fast news can spread here...

t_ch: 不不不，这根本不是「信息流动速度」能解释的问题。
t_en: No, no, no, this isn't just about how fast information spreads.

t_ch: 这文章也是一系统时内写好的？
t_en: This article was written within a system hour?

t_ch: 仙舟科技那么发达，估计也有什么「快速写稿机」？
t_en: With the Xianzhou's advanced technology, I wouldn't be surprised if they have something like a "rapid drafting machine".

t_ch: 不不不，这里面还有多方采访呢！总不可能是「自动采访机」瞬间完成的吧？
t_en: That's impossible! There are multiple interviews in this! There's no way they have an "automatic interviewer machine" that can instantly do all the interviews!

t_ch: 这些新闻报道和采访稿，绝对不是临时准备的。这是蓄谋已久的恶意报道！
t_en: These reports and interviews were clearly not whipped up at the last moment. They're the result of a premeditated malicious report!

t_ch: 可他们也不知道我和托帕小姐见面的事啊？这…总不会是托帕小姐……
t_en: But they didn't know about my meeting with Miss Topaz, did they? It couldn't possibly be... Miss Topaz... behind this...

t_ch: 不，与托帕小姐无关，公司没必要做这种事。恐怕这位幕后黑手准备了很多个版本的报道，只是在等待合适的契机吧……
t_en: No, it's unrelated to Miss Topaz. The IPC has no reason to do this. The person behind this has likely prepared multiple versions of the report and was just waiting for the right opportunity...

t_ch: 如果你没去与托帕小姐见面，恐怕他们就会选择其他报道。
t_en: If you hadn't met with Miss Topaz, they probably would have opted for a different report.

t_ch: 卢卡选手，你是惹到什么人了吗？怎么会有人专门做这种事……
t_en: Luka, did you make an enemy? Why else would someone go to such lengths to do this...

t_ch: 我怎么会惹…啊！糟糕……
t_en: How could I have possibly made an enemy... Oh no!

t_ch: 莫非，这就是「盘外招」？
t_en: Could this be the "dirty trick?"

t_ch: 这是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 托帕小姐说，我们接下来的对手——铄金，很擅长搞一些「盘外招」。这恐怕就是他的手笔。
t_en: Miss Topaz mentioned that our next opponent, Shuojin, is known for using dirty tricks. This must be his doing.

t_ch: 我这就去讨个说法。
t_en: I'm going to confront him about this.

t_ch: 千万别去！现在去找他麻烦，完全是授人以柄……
t_en: Don't! Confronting him now would only make you fall into his trap!

t_ch: 接下来怎么办？
t_en: What should we do next?

t_ch: 说实话，面对汹涌的舆论，个人就是没什么特别好的应对方法……
t_en: Honestly, there's no good way to handle the tidal wave of public opinion.

t_ch: 我先想办法找人辟谣吧…但辟谣的速度永远比不上造谣的速度——那家伙就是利用这一点，想让你溺死在舆论之中。
t_en: I'll try to find someone to debunk the rumors... but the speed of debunking always lags behind lies — he's counting on that to drown you in public opinion.

t_ch: 唉…真是头疼。不过也无所谓，只要接下来我在赛场上堂堂正正地击败铄金，观众们自然就会知道他说的那些全都是谎言。
t_en: *sigh*... So annoying. But it doesn't matter. As long as I can defeat Shuojin fair and square in the upcoming match, the spectators will all see that everything he said was a lie.

t_ch: 卢卡选手…事情恐怕不会像你想的那么顺利。负面印象一旦形成，就没那么容易恢复了……
t_en: Luka... things might not turn out the way you hope. Once a negative perception sets in, it's difficult to reverse it...

t_ch: 好啦～卡美丽小姐，不要那么悲观。我卢卡哪有那么容易被盘外招击倒？一切都会好起来的！
t_en: Alright, don't be too pessimistic, Miss Carmella. I'm not so easily overcome by dirty tricks. Everything will work out!

t_ch: 我出去透透气，稍后回来再讨论这事。
t_en: I need some air. I'll be back later to discuss this.

t_ch: 看起来卢卡选手需要一些时间调整一下心态…我们在这里等等他吧。
t_en: It looks like Contestant Luka needs some time to clear his head... Let's wait for him here.

t_ch: 等待卢卡。
t_en: Wait for Luka.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: I've got other stuff to do.

t_ch: 想用流言蜚语击倒我？真是可笑！语言再怎么难听，还能比拳头硬吗？
t_en: Do you think you can bring me down with mere rumors? Ridiculous! Are words tougher than fists?

t_ch: 你看…那不是卢卡吗？
t_en: Look... isn't that Luka over there?

t_ch: 还真是卢卡…我之前还是他的粉丝呢！
t_en: It is Luka... I was actually his fan!

t_ch: 哈哈，这下喜欢过卢卡成了你的「案底」了！
t_en: No way! Being a former fan of Luka is basically like being a cyber criminal!

t_ch: 他们是在…讨论我？
t_en: Are they... talking about me?

t_ch: 他看过来了！你别那么大声！
t_en: He's noticed us! Keep it down!

t_ch: 你看，那就是卢卡吧！
t_en: Look, that's definitely Luka!

t_ch: 看模样是个挺阳光的小伙子，怎么背地里……
t_en: He looks like a decent guy. How could he be scheming behind the scenes...

t_ch: 我觉得他不是坏，就是单纯的蠢。小地方来的，还是习武出身，估计文化水平也不太行。
t_en: I don't think he's bad, just a bit naive. He's from a small town and has a background in martial arts, probably not the most cultured.

t_ch: 蠢就是坏！这两者有什么区别吗？
t_en: Naive is just as bad! What's the difference?

t_ch: 两位朋友，我得罪过你们吗？有必要说得这么难听吗？
t_en: Friends, have I wronged you in any way? Why speak so harshly of me?

t_ch: 糟糕，他听见了，快跑！
t_en: Oh no, he heard us, run!

t_ch: 别走啊！我们好好聊聊嘛！
t_en: Don't run! Let's just talk!

t_ch: 那不是卢卡吗？
t_en: Isn't that Luka over there?

t_ch: 真的诶…你听说那件事了吗？
t_en: Really? Did you hear about that incident?

t_ch: 听说了…真没想到……
t_en: I heard... it was unexpected...

t_ch: 为什么每个人都在谈论我…我到底做错了什么？
t_en: Why is everyone talking about me... What exactly did I do wrong?

t_ch: 你听说卢卡那件事了吗？
t_en: Did you hear about what happened with Luka?

t_ch: 真没想到卢卡是这样的人！
t_en: I never thought Luka would be like this!

t_ch: 卢卡伪装得真好啊！
t_en: Luka has fooled us all!

t_ch: 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡
t_en: Luka Luka Luka

t_ch: 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡
t_en: Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka

t_ch: 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡 卢卡
t_en: Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka Luka

t_ch: 停！都别说了！停！停！都闭嘴！
t_en: Stop! Everyone stop talking! Stop! Stop! Just shut up!

t_ch: 糟糕，一定是幻觉又发作了……
t_en: Oh no, it must be the hallucinations acting up again...

t_ch: 卢卡，公司到底给了你多少钱啊？和大家说说呗？
t_en: Luka, how much money did the IPC give you? Tell us about it!

t_ch: 我没拿公司一分钱！我已经拒绝了！
t_en: I haven't taken a single cent from the IPC! I refused their offer!

t_ch: 战略投资部一直对贝洛伯格贼心不死，卢卡，你也打算趁机捞一笔吧？
t_en: The Strategic Investment Department has always coveted Belobog. Luka, were you planning to take advantage of this opportunity as well?

t_ch: 胡扯！我对贝洛伯格从来没有过半点不忠！
t_en: Nonsense! I have never been disloyal to Belobog!

t_ch: 可惜没有人会相信你。他们只会相信我传达给他们的真相。
t_en: Sadly, no one will believe you. They will only believe the truth I convey to them.

t_ch: 你是…铄金？
t_en: Are you... Shuojin?

t_ch: 初次见面。之后我们应该还会在赛场上重逢吧…还是说，你打算识趣一些，就此退赛？
t_en: Nice to meet you for the first time. We'll probably meet again on the field... unless you plan to bow out gracefully now.

t_ch: 我不会退赛，人们也不会一直被你愚弄。我会在赛场上证明自己，他们有自己的判断力。
t_en: I won't drop out, and people won't keep being fooled by you. I'll prove myself on the field, and they can judge for themselves.

t_ch: 不，绝大多数人只会误把别人的观点当做自己的观点——只要大家都说你是可恶的，那么他们就会「认为」你是可恶的。
t_en: No, most people simply adopt others' perspectives as their own. If everyone says you're vile, then they'll believe you're vile.

t_ch: 歪理邪说。
t_en: That's not true!

t_ch: 那就来证明我错了吧——用你的拳头，用你唯一擅长的方式。
t_en: Prove me wrong with your fists, then — the only way you know how.

t_ch: 求之不得！
t_en: Wouldn't have it any other way!

t_ch: 卢卡选手！我只是来和你打个招呼而已，你竟然不讲武德，暴起伤人！
t_en: Luka! I just came to say hello, and you attack without honor!

t_ch: 难道你不敢在赛场上和我堂堂正正比一场，非得像这样用一些阴损的盘外招吗？
t_en: Scared to fight me fair and square in the arena? Must you resort to these dirty tricks?

t_ch: 就是就是！卢卡你怎么不讲武德！
t_en: Exactly! Luka, why aren't you playing fair!

t_ch: 分明是你在用盘外招，特意来这里挑衅我，怎么变成我不讲武德了！
t_en: It's obvious that you're using dirty tricks to provoke me. How did it end up being my lack of sportsmanship?

t_ch: 人家铄金都受伤了！怎么会是人家挑衅的你！
t_en: Shuojin is the one who got injured! How could it be him taunting you?

t_ch: 你们还讲不讲道理了？
t_en: Are you guys even reasonable anymore?

t_ch: 卢卡，你还好吗？
t_en: Luka, how do you feel?

t_ch: 我又出现幻觉了…这次还挺严重的……
t_en: I'm hallucinating again... This time it's pretty serious...

t_ch: 那走吧，我们再去一趟丹鼎司。
t_en: Let's head to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 不许走！他不讲武德，偷袭我！我们这道理还没说清楚呢！
t_en: Don't go! He has no honor. He ambushed me! We haven't sorted this out yet!

t_ch: 就是就是！先把道理讲清楚再走！
t_en: Exactly! Let's sort this out before he leaves!

t_ch: 把路让出来吧。还是说你们要先跟我的球棒讲讲道理？我可不是参赛选手，不受你们的道德绑架。
t_en: Make way. Or do you need to discuss this with my baseball bat? I'm not a contestant, and I'm not bound by your morals.

t_ch: 这…是他要讲道理，又不是我们要讲道理，你跟我们喊什么？
t_en: It's him who wants to argue, not us. Why are you yelling at us?

t_ch: 呃，那个…大家都散了吧！今天这件事就先算了吧。毕竟我也是个有担当的武术家，不能和宵小之辈纠缠。
t_en: Everyone... let's just disperse! Let's drop it for today. After all, as a responsible martial artist, I shouldn't be entangled with insignificant individuals.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}教练。
t_en: Thank you, Coach {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 人群一哄而散，你带着卢卡再度来到了丹鼎司。
t_en: The crowd disperses, and you take Luka to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}和卢卡，你们又来了呀～卢卡，妾身的梦貘对你有帮助吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME} and Luka, here you are! Luka, has the tuskpir been helpful to you?

t_ch: 非常感谢，那只梦貘对我很有帮助，这些天睡眠质量变好了很多……
t_en: Thank you. That tuskpir has been helpful, and my sleep quality has improved a lot...

t_ch: 但是今天…我又出现了很强烈的幻觉……
t_en: But today... I had a very strong hallucination...

t_ch: 你简单地讲了讲卢卡遭遇的困境。
t_en: You briefly describe the predicament Luka is facing.

t_ch: 嗯…看完那篇报道之后，我走在路上总能听见别人在讨论我的事…后来不知不觉陷入了幻觉，还在幻觉里和人大打出手……
t_en: Hmm... after reading that article, I can't stop overhearing people discuss me on the street... Later, I found myself slipping into a hallucination and ended up in a fight...

t_ch: 别担心，这只是精神压力引发了神经衰弱，进一步加重了你的幻觉。
t_en: Don't worry. It's just the mental stress causing neurotic exhaustion, further intensifying your hallucinations.

t_ch: 灵砂让丹士送来了香炉。她点燃香薰，和缓的烟气蔓延开来。
t_en: Lingsha had the alchemist bring over an incense burner. She lights the aromatherapy, and soothing smoke spreads.

t_ch: 你们在这里稍微休息一下吧。要是一直处在备赛的紧张状态中，对身体也不好～
t_en: Rest here for a bit. Being in a constant state of pre-competition tension isn't good for your health.

t_ch: 真难受…感觉大家都对我有误会，我想辩解，他们也不听……
t_en: It feels terrible... it seems like everyone misunderstands me. I try to explain, but they won't listen...

t_ch: 有什么好辩解的？原本相信你的人，不需要听你的辩解，打定主意不相信你的人，又不会听你的辩解。
t_en: What is there to explain? Those who initially believed in you don't require your explanations, and those who are determined not to believe won't listen to your explanations.

t_ch: 难道就让舆论这么乱说吗？
t_en: Are we just going to let the public opinion slander me like this?

t_ch: 舆论这东西嘛…和这香炉里的烟一样，只会随着风飘来飘去。风向总会变的，不是所有的风都会吹向你。
t_en: Public opinion... it's like the smoke in this incense burner, drifting with the wind. The wind will eventually shift, and not all of it will blow against you all the time.

t_ch: 你觉得周围的人都在议论你，是因为你身处竞锋舰的擂台附近。
t_en: You might think everyone is talking about you, but that's because you're near the Skysplitter arena.

t_ch: 可放眼整个罗浮…大部分人都不认识一个参加演武仪典的域外拳手吧？
t_en: But... Looking at all of the Luofu... Most of them would not know about an outworlder boxer participating in the Wardance.

t_ch: 妾身当初因为一些旧事，在罗浮丹鼎司内也饱受流言的困扰，不得不随师父一起远渡他乡……
t_en: Years ago, I faced rumors within the Luofu's Alchemy Commission and had to travel far with my master.

t_ch: 可事到如今，这些都已烟消云散。而我，回到了罗浮成为丹鼎司的掌事者。
t_en: Those issues have dissipated into thin air over time. Now, I've returned to the Luofu to oversee the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 所以您的意思是，好好打这场「邀请赛」，用实力证明自己？
t_en: Do you mean I should simply fight well in this invitational match and prove myself with my skills?

t_ch: 妾身以为，这是你当下能实实在在应对流言的最好方法。
t_en: I believe this is the best way you can truly handle rumors right now.

t_ch: 怎么样？心情好些了吗？好些了就赶紧回去准备比赛吧！
t_en: How are you feeling now? Better? Get back and prepare for the match if you are feeling better!

t_ch: 我好多了。谢谢你，灵砂司鼎！
t_en: I feel much better. Thank you, Cauldron Master!

t_ch: 真是药到病除啊！
t_en: It's just like a magic cure!

t_ch: 要是谣言能用熏香驱散就好了。
t_en: If only rumors could be dispelled with incense.

t_ch: 别贫嘴，快回去准备比赛吧！要好好教训一下那个造谣的家伙哦～
t_en: Enough with the jokes. Get back to training for the match! Show that rumor-spreader what you're made of!

t_ch: 快回去准备比赛吧！要好好教训一下那个造谣的家伙哦～
t_en: Get back to your training quickly! It's time to show that rumor-spreader a lesson!

t_ch: 托帕小姐？你怎么来了？
t_en: Miss Topaz? What are you doing here?

t_ch: 听说你和铄金的比赛要开始了…我过来瞧瞧。
t_en: I heard your bout with Shuojin was starting soon. I thought I'd come by.

t_ch: 托帕小姐提议，她可以帮你解决铄金带来的麻烦。
t_en: Miss Topaz offers her assistance to help you handle the trouble caused by Shuojin.

t_ch: 这是我们自己惹上的无妄之灾，还是不要给公司添麻烦了吧。
t_en: We're responsible for this mess, and there's no need to involve the IPC.

t_ch: 如果我接受了托帕小姐的帮助，那就欠了你一个大人情。到时你再提起商务合作的事，我可不好拒绝了。
t_en: If I accept your help, Miss Topaz, I'll owe you a favor. It would be hard to say no to you later about any business matters.

t_ch: 别这么说。我可不单单是为了你的声誉，也是为了公司的声誉呀～
t_en: Don't put it that way. I'm not just helping you for your sake, but also for the IPC's reputation.

t_ch: 他如果只是造你的谣，那我确实不好插手，但这场风波不该将公司裹挟在其中。我现在出手，也是师出有名。
t_en: If he were merely spreading rumors about you, I wouldn't get involved. But this controversy should not taint the IPC. My intervention is completely justified.

t_ch: 况且无论如何，我都绝不会以「人情债」要挟你做出违心的判断，放心吧。
t_en: And I certainly wouldn't use a favor to coerce you into doing something you're uncomfortable with. Rest assured.

t_ch: 谢谢托帕小姐，心意我领了。但我也有我的处事原则，欠了人情债，就必须还。
t_en: Thank you, Miss Topaz, for your consideration. However, I abide by my principles — if I owe a favor, I must repay it.

t_ch: 刚才有人对我说，舆论就像香炉里的烟，只会顺着风飘。只要等到风向转变的时候，所有的谣言都会消退。
t_en: Someone once told me public opinion is like incense smoke, drifting with the wind. Just wait for the wind to change, and all these rumors will dissipate.

t_ch: 我现在应该做的，就是用我这双拳头，在擂台上证明自己。
t_en: Right now, I need to prove myself with my fists in the ring.

t_ch: 好吧，我明白了。我不会强求你接受任何帮助。
t_en: Alright, I understand. I won't insist on you accepting my help.

t_ch: 但是，怎么说呢…权当是过来人建议吧——想让烟雾散去，最快的方法是灭火。
t_en: However, if I may offer a piece of seasoned advice — the quickest way to clear the smoke is to put out the fire.

t_ch: 说的没错，只要肯等待，所有风波都会过去。但你需要等上很久，而时间是最昂贵的。
t_en: You're right. All storms pass eventually, but waiting takes time, and time is costly.

t_ch: 走了～改变主意记得叫我～
t_en: I'm off now. Should you change your mind, remember to call me.

t_ch: 卢卡选手，我觉得你刚才应该接受托帕小姐的帮助…{NICKNAME}教练，你怎么看？
t_en: Luka, I think you should have accepted Miss Topaz's help... Coach {NICKNAME}, what's your take?

t_ch: 应该接受她的帮助。
t_en: You should have accepted her assistance.

t_ch: 是啊！一般人遇到这种事，哪有机会动用战略投资部的资源来替自己解决麻烦啊？
t_en: Indeed! How often does one get the opportunity to use resources from the Strategic Investment Department to solve personal problems?

t_ch: 在擂台上证明自己就够了。
t_en: Just proving myself in the arena should suffice.

t_ch: 在擂台上能证明什么呢？舆论场上，立场大于事实、标签大于观点、感性大于理性，情感宣泄大于深度讨论…你要证明给谁看呢？
t_en: What really can be proven in the ring? In the court of public opinion, allegiances outweigh facts, labels trump perspectives, emotions eclipse logic, and outbursts overshadow deep discussions. Who are you trying to convince?

t_ch: 真相是不辩自明的。
t_en: The truth will always reveal itself.

t_ch: 抱歉，我实在没办法这么乐观……
t_en: I'm sorry. I can't be as optimistic...

t_ch: 别担心，卡美丽小姐。先待我赢回一场胜利，再谈之后的事吧。
t_en: Don't worry, Miss Carmella. Let me secure a victory, then we can discuss the future.

t_ch: 嗯…请一定要胜利呀！
t_en: Make sure you win!

t_ch: 亲爱的观众朋友们，欢迎来到星天演武仪典邀请赛的擂台！
t_en: Dear spectators and friends, welcome to the Luminary Wardance invitational match!

t_ch: 本场比赛对阵的两方，分别是风头正劲的明日之星——「铁臂」卢卡！
t_en: In this match, the two opposing sides are, the rising star — Luka "Strongarm"!

t_ch: 以及仙舟古武术的传承人——铄金！
t_en: And I'll introduce the inheritor of Xianzhou ancient martial arts — Shuojin!

t_ch: 卢卡退赛吧！
t_en: Luka, just withdraw!

t_ch: 卢卡我们不想看你赢！
t_en: Luka, we don't want to see you win!

t_ch: 呃…卢卡选手最近因为一些捕风捉影的传闻，受到了部分粉丝的敌视。
t_en: Ah... Luka has been subjected to some baseless hostility from a segment of his fans.

t_ch: 但是没关系，我相信卢卡选手一定能在赛场上证明自己！加油呀！卢卡选手！
t_en: But no worries. I'm confident Luka will demonstrate his true capabilities in the competition today! Go, Luka!

t_ch: 捕风捉影？哼，言之凿凿！
t_en: Baseless accusations? Absolute nonsense!

t_ch: 那个叽米就是公司的员工！他如此逆风局还向着卢卡说话，可见卢卡真的是公司走狗！
t_en: Owlbert is paid by the IPC! The fact that he still speaks well of Luka despite the controversy proves that Luka is just an IPC puppet!

t_ch: 我原以为叽米是个可爱的好鸟，结果也是公司的一丘之貉！
t_en: I used to think Owlbert was a decent character. But it turns out he's just another IPC stooge!

t_ch: 啊这…卢卡选手，再替你说话我恐怕要把自己也给搭进去了，你自求多福吧！
t_en: Ah... if I continue to defend you, Luka, I might end up implicating myself. You're on your own!

t_ch: 铄金，我不想和你多说一个字。就让我用拳头来向大家展示——我卢卡究竟是公司走狗，还是一名堂堂正正的格斗家吧！
t_en: Shuojin, I don't want to waste words with you. Let my fists show everyone — am I, Luka, just an IPC puppet, or am I a true fighter?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！公司走狗，我不会屈服于你的淫威！今日对决之后，你卢卡定会身败名裂。
t_en: Hahaha! IPC puppet, I won't bend to your intimidation! After today, you, Luka, will be utterly disgraced.

t_ch: 你这混小子，下手真狠啊……
t_en: You fight hard, don't you...

t_ch: 卢卡选手顶住巨大的压力，成功战胜了铄金选手！真是一场酣畅淋漓的大胜！
t_en: Under immense pressure, Luka triumphs over Shuojin in a thoroughly satisfying triumph!

t_ch: 让我们把掌声献给胜利者——卢卡选手！
t_en: Let's give a round of applause for the winner — Luka!

t_ch: 黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！
t_en: Rigged! Rigged! Rigged! Rigged! Rigged! Rigged! Rigged! Rigged!

t_ch: 我以为这演武仪典公平公正，谁想到也会有如此黑幕？
t_en: I thought this Wardance was supposed to be fair and just. Who would have imagined there was such rigging!

t_ch: 我是堂堂正正打赢的，哪里有什么黑幕？
t_en: I fought fairly and won! This isn't rigged!

t_ch: 哪里有黑幕！你们不要在这里胡说！
t_en: There's no rigging! Stop spreading falsehoods!

t_ch: 观众朋友们，没有黑幕。是我技不如人，肉身扛不住卢卡的铁拳…对不起，让你们失望了……
t_en: Dear spectators, this isn't rigged. I was simply outmatched and could not withstand Luka's iron fists... I'm sorry to have disappointed my supporters...

t_ch: 你这又是在演什么戏？
t_en: What act are you putting on now?

t_ch: 铁拳打肉身，想想都疼…卢卡下手真的太黑了，怎么会有这么恶毒的人啊？
t_en: Iron fist to flesh, just thinking about it hurts... Luka hits too hard. How can someone be so cruel?

t_ch: 能不能报警让地衡司把卢卡抓起来啊？
t_en: Can we call the Cloud Knights and have the Realm-Keeping Commission arrest Luka?

t_ch: 就是！我们不能让他就这么逍遥法外！
t_en: Exactly! We can't let him get away with this!

t_ch: 这…我分明用自己的实力赢得了比赛…怎么会变成这样……
t_en: I won the match fair and square... How did it come to this...

t_ch: 卢卡选手，此地不宜久留。快走吧，这里我来应付。
t_en: Luka, it's not safe to stay here. Go quickly, and I'll deal with this.

t_ch: 好好好，大家冷静一点！不要那么大的火气！这样吧～我给大家唱个歌吧～给大家翻唱个《在银河中孤独摇摆》怎么样？
t_en: Alright, alright, let's all calm down! No need to get so upset! How about this — I'll sing a song for everyone, a cover of Sway to My Beat in Cosmos. How does that sound?

t_ch: 片刻之后，你带着失魂落魄的卢卡离开了赛场。
t_en: Moments later, you leave the arena with the disheartened Luka.

t_ch: 明明凭实力打赢了，可是为什么…为什么事情会变成这样子呢？
t_en: I won fair and square, but... why did things turn out this way?

t_ch: 网上有人科普说雅利洛-Ⅵ是个已经灭亡的行星。可贝城的大家不是活得好好的吗！
t_en: Some online sources claim that Jarilo-VI is an extinct planet. But aren't the people of Belobog still alive?

t_ch: 他们还说我是个冒用身份的偷渡客…？喂，我可是正儿八经地从天舶司海关进入仙舟的啊！
t_en: They're saying I'm a stowaway using a fake identity... Hey, I entered the Xianzhou properly through the Sky-Faring Commission customs!

t_ch: 有人说我以前是混泯灭帮的…对了，卡美丽小姐，泯灭帮是做什么的啊？算了，你不用回答，听名字也知道这不是什么好东西。
t_en: Some even say I used to be part of the Annihilation Gang... Right, Miss Carmella, what does the Annihilation Gang do? Never mind. You don't have to answer, as the name tells me it's nothing good.

t_ch: 还有这个…有个评论说我的手是被仙舟剑士给剁了，所以才换上了现在的义肢，专挑罗浮的选手打压。
t_en: There's a comment claiming my arm was chopped off by a swordmaster from the Xianzhou, so I got my current prosthetics specifically to challenge fighters from the Luofu.

t_ch: 这样的玩笑……一点也不好笑！
t_en: Such jokes... are not funny at all!

t_ch: 卢卡，别看这些了，这对你现在的状态一点好处也没有。
t_en: Luka, stop looking at these. It's not helping your current state.

t_ch: 卡美丽说的没错。
t_en: Miss Carmella is right.

t_ch: 现在最好的做法是放下手机。
t_en: The best thing to do now is to put down your phone.

t_ch: 我明白！我知道自己现在该把所有人的评论当作一阵风那样。可是，我没法装作什么事都没发生啊！我看到一些支持我的人只是为我辩护了两句，就在网上遭到了围攻。
t_en: I understand! I know I should take everyone's comments lightly. But I can't pretend nothing happened! Some of my supporters briefly defended me, only to face backlash online.

t_ch: 在贝城的时候，我从没意识到「打赢比赛」这件事会伤害这么多人……
t_en: Back in Belobog, I never realized that winning a match could hurt so many people...

t_ch: 赛场上大家聚在一起的时候，确实都会为胜利者欢呼的。
t_en: When we are all together at the arena, everyone indeed cheers for the winner.

t_ch: 但比赛之外，尤其是在网上…可不是人人都会为胜者高兴的。毕竟，一直胜利在某些人看来也是一种傲慢。
t_en: But outside of the arena, especially online... not everyone is happy for the winner. After all, being continuously successful can be seen as arrogance to some.

t_ch: 但这也不代表所有人都希望看到你失败的，卢卡先生！你刚才说过，有支持者会为你辩护。在网上，不友善的声音听起来总会特别响亮刺耳，让支持你的声音反而显得微不足道。
t_en: But that doesn't mean everyone wants to see you fail, Mr. Luka! There are supporters for you. Online, unfriendly voices always seem particularly loud and harsh, overshadowing the supportive voices.

t_ch: 何况…假如让你重来一遍今天的比赛，你会为了逃避这些刺耳的声音选择输给铄金吗？
t_en: Moreover... if you could redo today's match, would you choose to lose to Shuojin just to avoid these grating voices?

t_ch: 我……绝不会为了任何理由假装输给谁。
t_en: I... would never pretend to lose to anyone for any reason.

t_ch: 那就对了！你现在最应该做的就是收拾好心情，为那些还在支持你的人赢下更多的比赛。
t_en: That's right! What you should do now is get your spirits up and win more matches for those who are still supporting you.

t_ch: 卡美丽小姐，教练，我要接着开始今天的训练了。
t_en: Miss Carmella, Coach, I'm going to start training for today.

t_ch: 不愧是「铁臂卢卡」，没想到你不仅接下了对手的拳，还扛住了这波舆论攻势。
t_en: Truly Luka the Strongarm! Not only did you take on your opponent's punches, but you weathered this wave of public opinion too.

t_ch: 是…是托帕小姐，欢迎大驾光临！
t_en: It's... Miss Topaz, welcome! Great to have you here!

t_ch: 卡美丽，{NICKNAME}，还有卢卡，你们好啊，我来看看卢卡选手的状态如何。
t_en: Hello, Miss Carmella, {NICKNAME}, and Luka. I came to check on how Luka is doing.

t_ch: 只是来看看状态？
t_en: Just checking in?

t_ch: 当然…不只是为了这个。我来是为了送一封活动邀请函给卢卡选手。
t_en: Not just for that. I'm here to deliver an invitation to Luka for an event.

t_ch: 卢卡应该已经拒绝过代言了。
t_en: Luka has already refused endorsements.

t_ch: 当然，我并不是为了代言的事来的。以网络上的流言蜚语来看…他和公司现在不应该、也不适合缔结商业合作，这会让双方都无利可图的。
t_en: I know. I'm not here about the endorsement. Given the online rumors... he and IPC shouldn't, and couldn't enter into a business partnership now, as it would be unprofitable for both sides.

t_ch: 所以，我这次来之是为了送一封活动邀请函给卢卡。
t_en: My visit is purely to deliver an event invitation to Luka.

t_ch: 活动邀请？
t_en: An event invitation?

t_ch: 借着演武仪典的风头，公司打算举行的一次小小的产品发布活动，就在这座竞锋舰上。如果能请到卢卡选手到场参加，会是一件十分荣幸的事情——
t_en: Riding on the momentum of the Wardance, the IPC plans to hold a small product launch event here on the Skysplitter. It would be an honor if Luka could attend.

t_ch: 这样一来岂不是坐实了网上的流言？他们可是把我喷成公司豢养的狗了……
t_en: Wouldn't that just validate the online rumors? They've been smearing me as the IPC's lapdog...

t_ch: 我发出邀请，正好能解决这个让你头疼的麻烦。
t_en: My invitation could solve this headache for you.

t_ch: 我们签下了一位新的代言人，会在这一次的产品发布活动上正式亮相宣布。
t_en: We've signed a new ambassador, who will make an official appearance at this product launch.

t_ch: 卢卡出现在活动现场，但代言人却不是他，人们对他的无端猜想就会不攻自破，要不了多久流言也会烟消云散的，对吧？
t_en: Luka showing up at the event, but not as the ambassador, would dispel baseless speculations, and soon the rumors will dissipate.

t_ch: 顺带说一句，我给你带了一只全新的战斗义肢作为礼物——我注意到在之前的比赛中你的铁臂总有些轻微的制动失误，怕是磨损得厉害，不堪用了吧？
t_en: By the way, I brought you a new combat prosthetic as a gift. I noticed some slight malfunctions in your iron arm during the last match. The wear and tear is probably so much that the arm is no longer suitable for use, I presume?

t_ch: 你不肯拿公司的钱，我也很头疼，所以我只好用这样的小恩小惠来「收买」你了。
t_en: You refuse to take the IPC's money, which troubles me, so I have to "bribe" you with these little favors.

t_ch: 可是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 别问我「我该给你多少钱」这样伤人的问题。记得换上它，好好迎接你的下一场比赛。
t_en: Don't bring up anything about the prosthetic's cost. That'll hurt my feelings. Make sure you gear up and get ready for your next match.

t_ch: 时候不早了，我还得去筹备发布会。那么，就此别过吧。
t_en: It's getting late. I still need to prepare for the launch event. Farewell for now.

t_ch: 托帕小姐，请留步！你…为什么要帮我解决麻烦？
t_en: Miss Topaz, wait! Why are you helping me solve these troubles?

t_ch: 你们雅利洛人的自尊还真是和雪地里的石头一样冰冷坚硬啊。你的潜台词是问我到底打什么算盘对吧？
t_en: Your Jarilo pride is as cold and hard as a rock in the snow. Are you trying to figure out what plans I've got?

t_ch: 我可是嗅到了阴谋的气息…
t_en: I smell a conspiracy...

t_ch: 喂！可别把公司的人都当作耍弄阴谋诡计的坏蛋啊。
t_en: Hey! Don't assume everyone at the IPC is out to play dirty.

t_ch: 托帕小姐本可以对处于舆论风口的卢卡先生不闻不问，装聋作哑。但她却不避嫌来发出邀请。没道理这样猜疑别人的好心吧？
t_en: Miss Topaz could have acted oblivious and ignored Mr. Luka, who is at the center of public scrutiny. Instead, she chose not to shy away from suspicion and extended the invitation. Isn't it unfair to question someone's good intentions?

t_ch: 没有的事，谢谢你，托帕。
t_en: No, not at all, thank you, Miss Topaz.

t_ch: 我尊敬无法被收买的人…或者说，贝洛伯格相关的一切是由我负责的项目，我要为它的长远利益考虑。你想选哪个理由说服自己都可以。明天见。
t_en: I respect those who can't be bought... or rather, everything related to Belobog is under my project, and I have to consider its long-term interests. Pick whichever reason you want to convince yourself. See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 第二天
t_en: The next day

t_ch: 欢迎两位。卢卡，你能抛开别人的眼光，出席这次的发布活动，我很高兴。
t_en: Welcome, both of you. Luka, I'm glad you could overlook the public scrutiny and join us for the event.

t_ch: 发布会马上要开始了。
t_en: The launch event is about to start.

t_ch: 随着一阵强而有力的音乐响起，主持人摇摆身子登上了擂台。
t_en: As powerful music plays, the host sways onto the stage.

t_ch: 女士们先生们、父老们乡亲们，不管咱们罗浮有没有昼夜的概念，总之先祝大家早上好中午好晚上好！我是你们最爱的主持人——叽米！
t_en: Ladies, gentlemen, elders, and community members! Whether the Luofu has a day-night cycle — good morning, good afternoon, and good evening! I'm your favorite host, Owlbert!

t_ch: 前日那场卢卡对铄金的比赛，想必让很多观众心中颇有愤懑吧？勇敢正直的铄金选手，怎么就输给了阴险狡诈的卢卡选手？这简直太令人意难平了！
t_en: The recent match between Luka and Shuojin likely left many spectators feeling outraged. How could the honorable and brave Shuojin lose to the cunning and deceptive Luka? It's simply too much to bear!

t_ch: 打倒卢卡！为铄金报仇！
t_en: Down with Luka! Avenge Shuojin!

t_ch: 报仇！报仇！报仇！
t_en: Avenge! Avenge! Avenge!

t_ch: 所以今天，在我老叽米的安排下，专门为铄金选手组织了一场「复仇赛」，而复仇的目标，自然就是——恶名昭彰的——「铁臂」——卢卡！
t_en: So today, under my arrangement, a rematch has been organized specifically for Shuojin. The target, of course, is the notorious Luka "Strongarm"!

t_ch: 啊？这是在搞什么名堂……
t_en: What is this all about...

t_ch: 卢卡下去吧！下去吧！下去吧！
t_en: Luka, get off the stage! Off the stage! off the stage!

t_ch: 各位稍安勿躁…接下来，则是即将上演复仇大戏的勇士，同时也是「次世代动力战斗装甲ARMS」的代言人，仙舟古武术传承者，星际和平公司产品代言人——铄金！
t_en: Everyone, please calm down... Coming up next is the warrior set to perform in this revenge play, the spokesperson for ARMS — Advanced Reinforced Mechanized Suit, the inheritor of Xianzhou's ancient martial arts, and the IPC's product ambassador — Shuojin!

t_ch: ……这铄金先生不是反对公司的传统武学派吗？怎么又接下了这代言机甲的活儿？
t_en: ...Isn't Mr. Shuojin a member of the traditional martial arts schools that oppose IPC? Why did he take on this mech endorsement?

t_ch: 我之前还在网上看他直播表演肉身击破公司机甲的绝活……怎么这会儿又来给公司的武器代言了？
t_en: I remember seeing him livestream his skills, breaking IPC mechs with his bare hands. How is he endorsing the IPC's weapons now?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊，这两边怎么穿一条裤子里去的，这是唱的哪一出啊？
t_en: I have no clue... How did these two end up on the same side? What's the deal with this?

t_ch: 各位请稍安勿躁！
t_en: Ladies and gentlemen, please remain calm!

t_ch: 有人看到我站在这擂台上也许会感到奇怪，认定我表里不一，在金钱和利益面前屈膝做了仙舟的叛徒。但我要告诉各位，事情绝不是各位所想的那样的！
t_en: Some of you might find it odd to see me up here and think I'm just selling out for money, betraying the Xianzhou. But that's not true!

t_ch: 最初公司找到我，要我为这款机甲代言时，我是拒绝的！铄某人苦练的仙舟古武术，那是正儿八经的非物质文化遗产，其强健体魄的功效远胜机甲百倍，是寰宇文明不可多得的瑰宝！
t_en: When the IPC first approached me to endorse this mech, I refused! The Xianzhou's ancient martial arts I've practiced diligently are a legitimate intangible cultural heritage, whose effects in strengthening the body are a hundred times superior to those of mechs, a rare treasure of universal civilization!

t_ch: 所以，你现在到底为什么站在这儿啊？要是被绑架了你就快眨眨眼啊，铄金先生！
t_en: Why are you standing there? If you're being coerced, just blink quickly, Mr. Shuojin!

t_ch: 咳咳，但各位也许没听说过，我们这一系武学与星际和平公司机甲有源远流长的关系。
t_en: Ahem, but you might not know that our martial arts tradition has a long-standing relationship with IPC mechs.

t_ch: 那么有人就要问了，这仙舟古武术怎么就能和星际和平公司扯上关系呢？这话当从两千多年前说起。有史载为证，博识学会曾受邀相助仙舟参与丰饶民战争……
t_en: So, you might wonder, how do Xianzhou ancient martial arts connect with the IPC? Well, this story goes back to over two thousand years ago. Historical records show that the Intelligentsia Guild was once invited to help the Xianzhou during the Denizens of Abundance Wars...

t_ch: 彼时有博识学会的智者效仿仙舟金人，造出十三套动力甲，并悉心改良了金人格斗术用于盟助仙舟——这，便是铄某人所用古武术之由来。
t_en: Back then, the wise men of the Intelligentsia Guild modeled their creations after the Xianzhou's aurumaton, crafting thirteen sets of powered armor and meticulously refining aurumaton combat techniques to aid the Xianzhou — this is the origin of the martial arts I practice.

t_ch: 可叹年悠月久，这段盟友佳话竟然被后人遗忘。这也是我今天出现在这里的原因，我要还原历史，重现历史。星际和平公司的机甲，也是咱们仙舟古武术的瑰宝！
t_en: Regrettably, over the years, this tale of allies has been forgotten. This is why I'm here today, to restore and bring back history. The Interastral Peace Corporation's mechs are also treasures of Xianzhou ancient martial arts!

t_ch: 没想到啊没想到，公司的动力甲竟然与仙舟有如此深厚的关系——
t_en: Who would have thought the IPC's powered armor has such a deep connection with the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 公司的机甲和你的武术同根同源是吧？之前你怎么不想起来？是公司用钱唤醒你的记忆了吗？
t_en: The IPC's mechs and your martial arts share the same roots? Why didn't you mention this before? Did the IPC's money help you remember?

t_ch: 到现在了你才跟我们说这个？你对得起我们这些支持你的老粉吗，铄金？！
t_en: And you're telling us this now? How are you going to face us, your longtime supporters, Shuojin!?

t_ch: 铄金，滚蛋！铄金，滚蛋！铄金，滚蛋！
t_en: Shuojin, scram! Shuojin, scram! Shuojin, scram!

t_ch: 各位且慢震怒！为了证明铄某所言非虚，今天我将穿上这套装甲为各位演示一番，让各位瞧瞧我的一招一式配上这公司铁甲是否是天衣无缝！
t_en: Everyone, calm down! To prove that my words aren't empty, today I will put on this armor and demonstrate for all of you. Let's see if my every move seamlessly matches with this IPC armor!

t_ch: 各位！在之前的演武仪典上，我以一招之差，惜败于来自贝洛伯格的卢卡选手！恰好他今天到访，铄某人冒昧向他再度发起挑战，不知卢卡选手是否愿意再指点我一番？
t_en: Ladies and gentlemen! In the previous Wardance, I lost to Luka from Belobog by a narrow margin! Coincidentally, he is here today, and I boldly challenge him again. Luka, would you be willing to teach me a move or two once more?

t_ch: 这该不会是托帕安排的吧？
t_en: Could this be arranged by Miss Topaz?

t_ch: 你四下环顾，却没有找到托帕的影子。
t_en: You look around but can't find Miss Topaz.

t_ch: 卢卡，别接受他挑战…
t_en: Luka, don't accept his challenge...

t_ch: 我要是接下了铄金的挑战却被打败了，他就找回了演武擂台上落败的面子……
t_en: If I accept his challenge and lose, he'll regain his honor from his previous defeat in the ring...

t_ch: 但要是我拒绝了他的挑战，恐怕要落个拳台上不战而逃的名声，被这位铄金先生当作大肆宣传的材料。
t_en: But if I refuse his challenge, I'll likely earn infamy for fleeing the ring without a fight, which Shuojin will surely use as propaganda.

t_ch: 打就打，谁怕谁？
t_en: Bring it on!

t_ch: 说的不错。要是拒绝他的挑战，恐怕要落个拳台上不战而逃的名声，被这位铄金先生当作大肆宣传的材料。
t_en: Well said. If you refuse his challenge, you might gain infamy for fleeing the ring, and Shuojin would use it as propaganda.

t_ch: 所以……这就是托帕小姐所说的「解决问题」的法子吗？她把机会递到了我的面前，我也得牢牢把握才行。
t_en: Is this Miss Topaz's way of solving problems? She's handed me the opportunity, and I need to grasp it firmly.

t_ch: 铄金先生，你的挑战，我接下了！
t_en: Shuojin, I accept your challenge!

t_ch: 好家伙！卢卡选手干脆利落应下了这场战斗！
t_en: Alright! Luka decisively accepts the fight!

t_ch: 铄金选手究竟能否一雪前耻，身着公司提供的动力甲，击落来自贝洛伯格的明日之星，以他独门武学挽回昨日的败局呢？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: Will Shuojin be able to redeem his previous defeat, wearing IPC powered armor and using his unique martial arts to turn yesterday's defeat around? Let's see!

t_ch: …呜，怎么可能。公司的最新款动力甲，没道理打不过你这乡巴佬的原始科技……
t_en: ...Ugh, how is this possible? The IPC's latest technology shouldn't be outmatched by your primitive technology...

t_ch: 这一定是黑幕，没错！是黑幕！是公司的黑幕！观众朋友们，这卢卡一定是和公司勾结起来，用了什么见不得人的手段，才能像这样打败我的！
t_en: This must be rigged! It's rigged! It's The IPC's rigging! Dear spectators and friends, Luka must have colluded with the IPC and used some sneaky tactics to beat me!

t_ch: 你在说什么胡话呢？和公司勾结的不就是你吗？
t_en: What nonsense are you talking about? Isn't it you who's colluded with the IPC?

t_ch: 铄金先生，看来这次，要身败名裂的人是你了。
t_en: Shuojin, it seems this time, the one to be disgraced is you.

t_ch: 看来比赛的结果已经尘埃落定！本场比赛的胜利者，乃是万众瞩目的超级巨星、磐岩的冠军——「铁臂」卢卡！
t_en: It seems the outcome of the match is settled! The winner of the match, the superstar in the spotlight, and the champion from Belobog — Luka "Strongarm"!

t_ch: 接下来，也将为各位披露星际和平公司将在本次发布会上隆重推出的全新产品——「玛米图<unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus战斗用外骨骼」！
t_en: Next, we will unveil the new product that the Interastral Peace Corporation is proudly introducing at this launch event — Mamitu <unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus Combat Exoskeleton!

t_ch: 没错，现在卢卡选手所佩戴的那一款，正是我们所开发的新产品！卢卡选手，举起你那胜利的手臂，向大家展示一下吧！
t_en: Yes, the model that Luka is wearing right now is our newly developed product! Luka, raise your victorious arm and show it off to everyone!

t_ch: 稍等一下，这怎么变成我义肢的发布会了……
t_en: Hold on. How did this turn into my prosthetic's launch event...

t_ch: 是啊，托帕小姐？托帕！你跑到哪里去了！这怎么变成他的发布会了？这不是我的发布会吗？托帕小姐，这跟我们说好的不一样啊？
t_en: Indeed! Miss Topaz? Miss Topaz! Where did you go? How did this turn into his launch event? Isn't this supposed to be my event? Miss Topaz, this isn't what we agreed on, is it?

t_ch: 首先，这既不是「你的」发布会，也不是「他的」发布会，这是公司产品的发布会。其次，这和说好的一模一样，铄金先生——这场发布会，将属于获胜的产品。
t_en: Firstly, this is neither "your" nor "his" event. This is a product launch for the IPC. Secondly, everything is exactly as agreed, Mr. Shuojin — this event belongs to the winning product.

t_ch: 托帕！你这个阴险歹毒的公司狗！我是不会放过你的！我要宰了你！
t_en: Topaz! You treacherous, ruthless corporate lapdog! I won't let you get away with this! I'm going to take you down!

t_ch: 保安，清理一下。
t_en: Security, clear the area.

t_ch: …正如各位刚才看到的那样！卢卡选手使用「玛米图<unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus战斗用外骨骼」，甚至击败了公司最新研发的「次世代动力战斗装甲ARMS」！真是令人大跌眼镜的矛盾大对决！
t_en: ...As you just witnessed, Luka used Mamitu <unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus Combat Exoskeleton to defeat the IPC's latest Advanced Reinforced Mechanized Suit (ARMS)! Truly a jaw-dropping clash of contradictions!

t_ch: 可是，我两只手臂都还在，该怎么使用这机械义肢呢？
t_en: But I still have both arms. How am I supposed to use this mechanical prosthetic?

t_ch: 请各位放心，「玛米图<unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus战斗用外骨骼」现提供免费客制化服务，为不同身体结构的客户量身定做适合自己的外骨骼！
t_en: Rest assured. Mamitu <unbreak>17</unbreak>ProPlus Combat Exoskeleton now offers free customization services, tailor-made for customers with different body structures!

t_ch: 那么，哪里才能买得到呢？
t_en: So, where can one purchase it?

t_ch: 星际和平商城各大门店均有销售！我老叽米还给大家争取到了<unbreak>100</unbreak>个免费体验名额，今天在发布会现场和我一起高呼兑换码——「铁拳卢卡！无敌拳法！」即有机会领取！
t_en: Available at all major outlets of the Interastral Peace Mall! I, Owlbert, have secured <unbreak>100</unbreak> free trial spots for everyone. Today at the launch event, chant the redemption code "Iron Fist Luka! Iron Fist Fury!" to claim your spot!

t_ch: 免费体验？不得了了，公司难得要发善心了！有便宜不占是王八蛋！
t_en: A free trial? Don't miss out — The IPC is rarely this generous! It would be foolish to pass up such a bargain!

t_ch: 「铁拳卢卡！无敌拳法！」
t_en: Iron Fist Luka! Iron Fist Fury!

t_ch: 「铁拳卢卡！无敌拳法！」
t_en: Iron Fist Luka! Iron Fist Fury!

t_ch: 「铁拳卢卡！无敌拳法！」
t_en: Iron Fist Luka! Iron Fist Fury!

t_ch: 「铁拳卢卡！无敌拳法！」
t_en: Iron Fist Luka! Iron Fist Fury!

t_ch: 明明之前还都在骂我…这场面还真是…令人感慨啊……
t_en: They were still cursing me earlier... This turn of events is truly something to ponder.

t_ch: 是啊～但也令人心情舒畅，不是吗？
t_en: Yes, but it's quite satisfying, isn't it?

t_ch: 嗯…心情有点复杂。
t_en: Hmm... my feelings are mixed.

t_ch: 这里太吵了，卢卡，咱们换个地方聊聊吧。
t_en: It's too noisy here, Luka. Let's talk somewhere else.

t_ch: 伴随着巨浪般的欢呼，托帕与卢卡来到了上层擂台边。
t_en: Amidst the roaring cheers, Miss Topaz and Luka move to the upper tier of the arena.

t_ch: 怎么样，卢卡选手，对我的安排还满意吗～
t_en: How do you like my arrangement, Luka?

t_ch: 虽然让铄金露出了真面目…但，托帕小姐，下次还是请把安排事先告诉我吧！
t_en: Although it exposed Shuojin's true colors... Miss Topaz, please inform me about the arrangements in advance next time!

t_ch: 我不懂，铄金那个家伙，讨厌公司到骨子里去了，怎么会这么轻易地上钩，答应你的合作邀请？
t_en: I don't get it. Shuojin despises the IPC to the core. How could he fall for it and agree to your cooperation offer?

t_ch: 嗯？铄金恨公司吗？未必吧～
t_en: Does Shuojin really hate the IPC? I think perhaps not...

t_ch: 发布会开始前<unbreak>12</unbreak>系统时
t_en: <unbreak>12</unbreak> system hours before the launch event

t_ch: 广告代言？你这是什么意思？我可是现在整个罗浮反对公司资本介入仙舟武术的意见领袖。
t_en: Endorsement? What do you mean? I am currently the leading voice in the Luofu opposing the IPC's capital intervention in the Xianzhou martial arts.

t_ch: 战略投资部这次来到演武仪典的会场，不是为了争些无关紧要的比赛名次。以这场比赛为契机，获取更多的商业利益，才是我们的目的。铄金先生，相信你也是如此吧？
t_en: The Strategic Investment Department is here at the Wardance not for some trivial match rankings. Using this match as an opportunity to gain more business benefits is our goal. Mr. Shuojin, don't you feel the same?

t_ch: 对你我这样的人来说，道德、观点、立场都不过是给别人听的说辞。唯一重要的，只有数不清的信用点，不是吗？
t_en: For people like us, morality, perspectives, and allegiances are just pretenses for others. The only thing that matters is countless credits, isn't it?

t_ch: 呵…一个广告代言单子就想收买我？别傻了，我纵横拳台这么多年，难道还差这一点钱吗？而且我可早就听说你本想签下卢卡作为代言……
t_en: Do you think a mere endorsement deal can sway me? I've dominated the fighting scene for years. Do you think I'm that desperate for money? Besides, I've heard you were originally aiming to sign Luka as your spokesperson...

t_ch: 可惜，他不是个识时务的年轻人。他拒绝了我的提议。
t_en: Unfortunately, he's not a very smart young man. He refused my proposal.

t_ch: 有人拒绝双赢，就会有人接受。所以我找到了你。接下来，我将以「新产品发布会」的名义组织一场比武，比赛双方分别是，你和卢卡。
t_en: When someone refuses a win-win, someone else accepts. That's why I found you. Next, I will organize a duel under the guise of a new product launch, where you and Luka will compete.

t_ch: 我会为你准备最新款的公司装甲。凭你的本事，再加上公司最近研发的机甲，难道还惧怕一个独臂的拳击手吗？
t_en: I have prepared the latest IPC armor for you. With your skills, plus the IPC's newly developed mechs, are you afraid of a one-armed boxer?

t_ch: …这台公司的装甲，就是你们准备找我代言的东西？
t_en: ...This the IPC armor... is that what you want me to endorse?

t_ch: 没错。想一想，这次代言会带给你多大的好处？当众击败卢卡选手，被公司选为仙舟专属形象的代言人。
t_en: Bingo. Think about how beneficial this endorsement could be for you. Defeating Luka in public, and being chosen by the IPC as the exclusive face of the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 看到这块广告牌了吗？上面会印着你的脸，罗浮上的每个人都会认识你。
t_en: See this billboard? Your face will be on it, and everyone in the Luofu will know you.

t_ch: 印着我的脸…既然如此，那我还有个要求——我想做战略投资部相关业务管线的仙舟专员。
t_en: My face on a billboard... In that case, I have one more request — I want to be the specialist for all relevant businesses the Strategic Investment Department has with the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 公司欢迎不同文化、不同背景的新面孔。前提是：你在明天的发布会上，能赢过卢卡。
t_en: The IPC welcomes new faces from different cultures and backgrounds. That can be done — provided that you beat Luka at tomorrow's launch.

t_ch: 哈哈，你是瞧不起我铄金，还是瞧不起你们公司的机甲？
t_en: Are you underestimating me, or your company's mechs?

t_ch: 看来我们谈妥了～
t_en: It seems we have reached an agreement.

t_ch: 合作愉快，托帕小姐。
t_en: Pleasure doing business, Miss Topaz.

t_ch: 哦对了…你一直标榜自己使用的是仙舟传统武术，这会不会和公司的机甲产品有些概念冲突？
t_en: Oh... Considering you've always emphasized your use of traditional Xianzhou martial arts, wouldn't that conflict conceptually with the IPC's mech products?

t_ch: 不用担心。咱们仙舟古武术兼容并包，海纳百川，就算给我一把爆能枪，我也能把它包装成仙舟古武术的兵击流派之一。
t_en: Don't worry. Xianzhou ancient martial arts are inclusive and comprehensive. Even if you handed me a blast gun, I could integrate it as one of the traditional martial styles of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 这么大的广告牌，印着我的脸…哎呀，真是的，想想就刺激。几百年了，终于混出头了。
t_en: Such a large billboard with my face on it... Ah, just the thought is thrilling. After hundreds of years, I've finally made it.

t_ch: 原来…是这么一回事。这下我实实在在欠了你一个人情。
t_en: So... that's what this is all about. Now I truly owe you one.

t_ch: 可别这么说，我只是在处理一个小小的舆论麻烦。铄金不该为了战胜你，将公司拖入舆论的风波。
t_en: Please don't say that. I'm just handling a minor public relations issue. Shuojin shouldn't drag the IPC into controversy just to defeat you.

t_ch: 所以我给了他一个机会——当着我的面拒绝我，旗帜鲜明地继续反对公司。可惜他没有做到。
t_en: So I gave him a chance — to reject me in my face, clearly continuing his opposition to the IPC. Unfortunately, he didn't take it.

t_ch: 但我实在弄不明白，你为什么要让我来击败你们自己的机甲产品呢？
t_en: But I don't understand why you wanted me to defeat your mech products.

t_ch: 你是说…「次世代动力战斗装甲ARMS」。哈哈，那不过是一点滞销的旧型号存货罢了。
t_en: You mean... Advanced Reinforced Mechanized Suit (ARMS)? That's just some old unsold stock.

t_ch: 连广告牌都准备好了，这代言合同，我看来是非签不可了？
t_en: With the billboard already prepared and the endorsement contract in place, I suppose I have to sign it, don't I?

t_ch: 别太有心理负担。现在演武仪典的选手们，除了某位通缉犯，也就只剩下你还没接受公司的赞助了。
t_en: Don't feel too pressured. Now, among the Wardance contestants, besides a certain wanted criminal, you're the only one left who hasn't accepted the IPC's sponsorship.

t_ch: 通缉犯？那是谁？
t_en: A criminal? Who's that?

t_ch: 别着急嘛～照这个势头，你很快就会遇到他的。
t_en: Don't worry. You'll probably meet him soon.

t_ch: 我还有其他事要忙，先走了。你就在这里慢慢欣赏自己的海报吧。
t_en: I have other matters to attend to and has to go. Enjoy admiring your poster here.

t_ch: 托帕小姐…真的很感谢你！
t_en: Miss Topaz... I appreciate your help!

t_ch: 别谢我，谢你自己吧～如果发布会上你输了，今天这里宣传的产品就是铄金和他的「新款机甲」了。
t_en: Don't thank me. Thank yourself. If you had lost at the launch, today's promoted product would be Shuojin and his new mech.

t_ch: 这「邀请赛」还真是凶险啊…若是没有托帕小姐救场，真不知道会变成什么样。
t_en: This invitational match was truly perilous... If it weren't for Miss Topaz's intervention, who knows what it could have turned into?

t_ch: 确实要感谢她。
t_en: We owe her a big thank-you.

t_ch: 嗯，我也必须更加尽心尽力地打拳了。毕竟拿了人家的钱，就得好好帮别人做宣传。
t_en: Yes, I must also do my best to fight harder. After all, having taken their money, I need to do my part in promoting their products.

t_ch: 公司的手段好吓人…
t_en: The IPC's methods are quite frightening...

t_ch: 看起来很吓人，仔细想想也还好。
t_en: They seem scary, but if you think about it, not so much.

t_ch: 托帕小姐的计划，看起来相当歹毒，但却是打小人不打君子的阳谋。如果铄金是个正直的人，靠这招就根本没办法让他进圈套。
t_en: Miss Topaz's plan, though seemingly malevolent, is a cunning strategy that targets the unworthy, not the noble. If Shuojin were a person of integrity, this tactic wouldn't have lured him into the trap.

t_ch: 托帕这是两边下注啊。
t_en: Miss Topaz is betting on both sides.

t_ch: 唉，也不能要求人家不计风险帮助我这样一个小人物啦……
t_en: Can't expect her to take risks for a nobody like me...

t_ch: 不管怎么说，比赛都还得继续。哎…真心希望下次不要遇到这么恶心的对手了。
t_en: Regardless, the competition must go on. Sigh... I hope we don't encounter such vile opponents next time.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，遇到了也不怕。「无敌的磐岩冠军」才不会被同一招击败两次哩！
t_en: Even if I do, the peerless Boulder Champion won't be defeated by the same move twice!

t_ch: 听说选了个仙舟的代言人
t_en: Heard they chose a Xianzhou ambassador.

t_ch: 公司终于还是开窍了
t_en: The IPC has finally seen the light.

t_ch: 活动还没开始吗？
t_en: Has the event not started yet?

t_ch: 快看，那不是卢卡吗？
t_en: Look, isn't that Luka?

t_ch: 他来这儿干什么？
t_en: What's he doing here?

t_ch: 我就说吧…他和公司有一腿
t_en: I told you... he's involved with the IPC.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您好，这里有一个您的包裹，请签收一下。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. There's a package for you. Please sign here.

t_ch: 包裹？你最近也没乱买东西啊…看着眼前硕大的游戏机，你的大脑开始飞速运转。
t_en: Package? You didn't buy anything recently... Your brain starts to whirl in overdrive as you look at the big arcade machine in front of you.

t_ch: 且不论一个没长手的广告牌，怎么搬动这么重的货物。自己最近是中了什么奖？还是梦游的时候误刷了丹恒的信用卡？
t_en: And this is on top of how a handless billboard managed to carry such a heavy item... Did you win something recently? Or did you accidentally swipe Dan Heng's credit card when you were sleepwalking?

t_ch: 难道是假面愚者的陷阱？该死，这群家伙肯定选的货到付款。
t_en: Could it be a trap from the Masked Fools? Darn it. Those Fools must have picked the option for cash on delivery for delivery fees.

t_ch: 最终，千言万语汇聚成一句话。
t_en: In the end, those countless words merge together into one sentence.

t_ch: 你到底是怎么搬的？
t_en: How did you carry this?

t_ch: 这是商业机密，{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: That's a business secret, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 如果商品无破损情况，我就先离开了，还有两百份包裹需要在这个系统时内派送——当然，这里是梦境，就算有破损也多半是美术设计。
t_en: If the item isn't damaged, then I need to go. There are still <unbreak>200</unbreak> packages I need to deliver before the next system hour — And since we're in the Dreamscape, any damage is probably a deliberate aesthetic choice anyway.

t_ch: 你打开游戏机，一串患有社恐的文字以惊人的速度从眼前逃离。但假如你拥有足够强大的动态视力，就能轻易看清它的内容。
t_en: You switch on the arcade machine, and strings of words afflicted with social phobia run away from your vision at a startling speed. However, had you owned a more powerful dynamic vision, you would have read its content with ease.

t_ch: 这是我做的游戏机，{NICKNAME}，希望你能从中享受到游戏的乐趣。
t_en: This is the arcade machine I made, {NICKNAME}. I hope you can experience the joy of video games with it.

t_ch: 斯蒂芬•劳艾德。
t_en: Stephen Lloyd.

t_ch: 你发现了一台刚开封的游戏机，虽然它并没有长眼睛，但你十分确信，你们的视线交汇在了一起。
t_en: You find an arcade machine that has just been set up. Though it doesn't have eyes, you are sure that your eyes met for a moment.

t_ch: 「嘿，朋友，你就不好奇，我这儿有什么刺激的游戏吗？」游戏机无声地向你发出邀请。
t_en: "Hey, friend. Aren't you curious about what kind of exciting games I have here?" The arcade machine silently invites you.

t_ch: 这是一段被「终末」带至此地的景象。当你穿过<color=#dbc291ff>「黄金与机械」</color>的战火，跨越<color=#dbc291ff>美梦雕琢的世界</color>后，终会抵达的未来。
t_en: This is a scene brought here by the Finality. This is the future you're destined to arrive at after weaving through the fires of war in <color=#dbc291ff>Gold and Gears</color> and traversing the <color=#dbc291ff>Dreamjoy Statue World</color>.

t_ch: 你可以在那之后回到这里，届时，你将见证完整的故事。
t_en: You can return here after you've done all that. By then, you will bear witness to the whole story.

t_ch: 当然，你也可以像现在这样，向前迈进一步，让视界与「终末」重合。因果逆转，未来的事物显现在你的眼前……
t_en: Or you can also do what you are doing right now — Take a step forward and let your horizon merge with the Finality. Cause and effect overturn, and the future appears before you...

t_ch: 底层逻辑检定完毕…未发现异常数据，尝试第<unbreak>1291</unbreak>次信息输入……
t_en: Underlying logic assessment complete... No data anomalies found. Attempting the <unbreak>1291</unbreak>st message input...

t_ch: 结论：未能与黑塔取得联系。
t_en: Conclusion: No contact established with Herta.

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 有好多黑塔！
t_en: So many Hertas!

t_ch: 我又来测试了。
t_en: I'm here to do testing again.

t_ch: 很遗憾在这种时候与你相会，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。如你所见，项目出现了原因不明的故障，测试也将暂时延期。
t_en: I apologize for meeting you here at this time, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. As you can see, the project has encountered an unknown error. The test will also be temporarily delayed.

t_ch: 逻辑：在模拟宇宙中出现了巨大的空洞，它无法被观测和干涉，却始终保持正常运行。
t_en: Logic: A massive void has appeared in the Simulated Universe. It cannot be observed or interfered with, yet it continues to operate within normal parameters.

t_ch: 根据烛墨学派的资料，这片空洞本属于第二次帝皇战争后，一段被称为「学派战争」的历史。
t_en: According to data from the Candelagraphos, this void originally belonged to a time in history named the "Scholars' Strife" after the second Emperor's War.

t_ch: 但从外部向内窥探，那里只有一片紊乱的「不可知域」。
t_en: But when observed from the outside, all that can be seen within is only a chaotic "Unknowable Domain."

t_ch: 黑塔呢？
t_en: What about Herta?

t_ch: 这正是我们当前面临的困境之一。
t_en: This is exactly one of the dilemmas we are currently facing.

t_ch: 对于「不可知域」的修复工作，黑塔女士表现出异常的热情，她自告奋勇地接入了模拟宇宙。
t_en: Madam Herta is displaying unusual excitement towards the reconstruction of the Unknowable Domain, and has volunteered to connect herself to the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 但在那之后不久，我们就失去了联系，她的人偶也尽数陷入宕机状态。
t_en: Shortly after that, we lost contact. All her puppets are currently in a state of complete shutdown.

t_ch: 结论：距离她进入「不可知域」，已过去<unbreak>33</unbreak>个标准时。
t_en: Conclusion: Thirty-three standard hours have passed since her entry into the Unknowable Domain.

t_ch: 逻辑：诚然，可以直接找到黑塔女士，从外部将她强制弹出。但很可惜，那并非轻易能到达的场所，我们的时间也不甚宽裕。
t_en: Logic: It is possible to directly locate Madam Herta and forcefully eject her externally. Unfortunately, the location in question is difficult to reach, and time is running short.

t_ch: 让我进去找她。
t_en: Let me go in and look for her.

t_ch: …事实上，这的确是现存的最佳解决方案。
t_en: ...Honestly, this is the most optimal and immediate solution.

t_ch: 还有其他办法吗？
t_en: Is there any other way?

t_ch: 根据现有状况推断，最佳的解决方案是——找到一位有充足经验的测试员，从内部找到黑塔女士，将她带出来。
t_en: Based on the current situation, the most optimal solution is to identify an experienced tester, locate Madam Herta internally, and facilitate her extraction.

t_ch: 逻辑：在信息不充足的前提下，如果我在接入后，陷入和黑塔女士相同的失联状态，或将导致情况向不可逆的方向恶化。
t_en: Logic: Given the limited information we have, if I were to connect and subsequently experience the same communication breakdown as Madam Herta, it could result in an irreversible deterioration of the situation.

t_ch: 相反，由我在外部维持与你的联系，监视系统的运行，至少能够保证你的个人安全。
t_en: On the contrary, if I were to maintain communication with you on the outside and monitor the system's operation, it would ensure your safety at the very least.

t_ch: 包赢的。
t_en: Bet.

t_ch: 早知道，还得是我。
t_en: I knew it. It has to be me.

t_ch: 只能硬着头皮上了。
t_en: I'll have to bite the bullet.

t_ch: 情况紧急，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，请务必尽快出发。
t_en: The situation is urgent, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, please depart immediately.

t_ch: 提问：关于「不可知域」产生的原因，是否找到了有益的线索？
t_en: Question: Were there any useful clues discovered regarding the cause of the Unknowable Domain?

t_ch: 有些头绪，但不多——博识学会的黑历史倒见着不少。
t_en: There are some clues, but not many — I saw much of the dark history of the Intelligentsia Guild.

t_ch: 学派战争距离现在，也就一百多个琥珀纪。照理说，作为直接参与者的烛墨学派，应该完整记录了事件的始末。
t_en: It's only been about a hundred Amber Eras since the Scholars' Strife. Logically, as a direct participant, the Candelagraphos should have thoroughly documented the entire event.

t_ch: 可当我把资料和「不可知域」内的历史进行比对，却发现，到处是缺漏和虚构。
t_en: But when I compared the data with the history in the Unknowable Domain, I discovered that there are numerous omissions and fabrications throughout.

t_ch: 逻辑：博识学会向来对「神秘」讳莫如深，尤其是这种重要史料，理应不会放任虚构史学家进行篡改。
t_en: Logic: The Intelligentsia Guild has always been extremely apprehensive regarding the Enigmata, especially concerning such important historical materials. They would not have permitted the History Fictionologists to tamper with it.

t_ch: 除非他们是有意为之，借「神秘」的手来藏匿不应存在的信息。
t_en: Unless they did it with the intention to use the Enigmata as a cover, to conceal crucial information that should not exist.

t_ch: 他们想藏住什么？
t_en: What are they hiding?

t_ch: 我最爱的吃瓜时间。
t_en: It's my favorite time — Watch the drama unfold.

t_ch: 虚构史学家真好用。
t_en: The History Fictionologists are such great tools.

t_ch: 学派间为了支配「权杖」的算力打得你死我活，或是私底下有不可告人的交易，这些都无关痛痒。真正值得藏起来的，只可能是一种东西——知识本身。
t_en: The schools shed blood over the allocation of the Scepters' computational power, or there could have been clandestine exchanges happening, but all are inconsequential. There's only one thing truly worth concealing — knowledge itself.

t_ch: 按照公开的说法，数千台「权杖」被学士们愚蠢的问题占满，耗尽算力，变成如今任人拍照打卡的景点，飘浮在宇宙中的垃圾。
t_en: According to the official report, thousands of Scepters were flooded by foolish questions from scholars, exhausting their computational power. Now, they have become mere photo-op spots for everyone, floating in space like debris.

t_ch: 我会信？那可是天才的造物！——螺丝咕姆，我们对「权杖」系统的模拟精度能提升到多少？
t_en: You think I'd believe that? That is a creation of a genius! Screwllum, how much more can we improve the simulated accuracy of the Scepter?

t_ch: 结论：按照之前的估测，如果把模拟宇宙的算力全部集中在一起，仿真度能达到<unbreak>71</unbreak>％以上。
t_en: Conclusion: According to the previous estimates, if we concentrate all the computational power of the Simulated Universe, we can increase the accuracy to <unbreak>71%</unbreak> or more.

t_ch: 够用了。诸位很清楚，我不是一个安于现状的人。我一直想突破既有的知识桎梏，寻到新的突破点，令全宇宙的知识总量再度跃升。
t_en: That's enough. I'm sure everyone here knows that I'm never one to accept the status quo. I've always sought to push beyond the existing boundaries of knowledge, find new breakthroughs, and advance the total knowledge of the universe multiple-fold.

t_ch: 这一次对「学派战争」的演算，正是为了这个目标。为此，我打算<color=#dbc291ff>为模拟宇宙引入第五位合作者</color>了。
t_en: The current extrapolation of the Scholars' Strife is precisely for that purpose. Therefore, I've decided to <color=#dbc291ff>add in the fifth collaborator for the Simulated Universe</color>.

t_ch: 我十分想见余清涂！
t_en: I really want to meet Yu Qingtu!

t_ch: 你找到波尔卡了？
t_en: You found Polka?

t_ch: 难道是爱吃香蕉的那位？
t_en: Could it be the one who loves to eat bananas?

t_ch: 我们把鲁珀特二世「复活」吧！
t_en: Let's revive Rubert II!

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 当然不是字面意思上的「复活」。收集学派战争的数据，在模拟宇宙仿造出完整的「权杖」系统，重现帝皇的外置思维单元。
t_en: Of course, I'm not literally "reviving" anyone — Gather all the data from the Scholars' Strife, replicate the entire Scepter system in the Simulated Universe, and recreate the Emperor's external cognitive unit.

t_ch: 然后，我们就能得到第五位天才的头脑。而我，将向他……她？不，应该说向它提出问题——正如当年「学派战争」的尾声，有人提出了那个{RUBY_B#孤波算法}最终被我解开的难题{RUBY_E#}一样。
t_en: Then, we will be able to obtain the brain of the fifth genius. Then I shall ask him... or her? No, should be "it"... a question, just like how someone asked at the end of the Scholars' Strife the {RUBY_B#solitary waves theory}question that I ultimately solved{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 虽然斯蒂芬和阮•梅女士并无异议，但我个人对此持保留意见。为一次研究加入太多目的，会极大分散研究的精力。
t_en: Although Stephen and Madam Ruan Mei are both agreeable, I personally have reservations. Introducing multiple objectives into a single study will significantly dilute the research focus and reduce efficiency.

t_ch: 保留意见就是没意见——「学派战争」、我的起点、{RUBY_B#鲁珀特二世}第五位天才{RUBY_E#}这一切就像首尾相嵌的锁链，螺丝咕姆，你可别跟我说你对这次演算不好奇。
t_en: Reserving judgment is as good as having no opinion — the Scholars' Strife, my beginnings, and {RUBY_B# Rubert II}the fifth genius{RUBY_E#} — These are all chains tightly linked to one other. Screwllum, you'd better not tell me you're not curious about this extrapolation.

t_ch: 如您所说，实事求是：我的分析权重中确实加入了百分之十五的好奇情感。
t_en: As you indicated, to be objective: My analysis does include a fifteen percent weightage of curiosity.

t_ch: 嘿，{NICKNAME}，你在黄金与机械的表现不错，应对鲁珀特的造物也有经验，而我现在又正好缺一个助手，你说这事儿巧不巧？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, your performance in Gold and Gears was decent. You are experienced in dealing with Rubert's creations too, and it looks like I'm lacking an assistant. Isn't this just the perfect coincidence?

t_ch: 黑塔直勾勾地望着你，多半不打算给你选择的权利。不过仔细想想，这可是和黑塔女士一起做研究的宝贵机会，你真的想拒绝吗？
t_en: Herta stares at you intently, likely not intending to offer you a choice. But, after careful thought, this is a precious opportunity to work with Madam Herta on a research project — do you really want to decline?

t_ch: 求之不得。
t_en: That's all I've ever wanted.

t_ch: 哈哈，我拒绝。
t_en: Haha, I refuse.

t_ch: 黑塔女士拒绝了你的拒绝。
t_en: Madam Herta refuses to accept your refusal.

t_ch: 就这么定了——多来收集「权杖」的数据，保证你会看到不少刺激的好东西。
t_en: Then this settles it. Do come and collect more Scepter data. I guarantee that you'll see many exciting things.

t_ch: 难以置信，一个只出现在「学派战争」时期的演算盲区，但到目前为止，二者却没表现出任何相关关系。
t_en: Unbelievable, an extrapolation blind spot that only appeared during the Scholars' Strife era — But these two factors have not shown any relationship with each other to this day.

t_ch: 失灵的设备，毫无规律的现象，无论对哪个时代的学者都堪称致命的打击。
t_en: Malfunctioning equipment and phenomena occurring without rhyme or reason are fatal blows to scholars of any era.

t_ch: 难道是铁墓？不，如果是它，影响不可能局限在模拟宇宙，整个空间站都会因它的力量而瘫痪。
t_en: Could it be Irontomb? No, if that was it, the effect would have extended beyond the Simulated Universe. The entire space station would have been incapacitated from its power.

t_ch: 是鲁珀特二世留下的后手吗？可他早就死透了，总不至于还能从模拟宇宙里钻出来。除非……咱们的演算足够逼真？不可能。
t_en: Could Rubert II have concocted a contingency plan up his sleeve? But he was already dead beyond measure — He couldn't possibly emerge from within the Simulated Universe... Unless... our extrapolation is actually sufficiently realistic? Impossible.

t_ch: 最坏的打算是，两个区域的重合，真就只是一场巧合。巧合，巧合也太多了……
t_en: The worst-case scenario is that the overlapping of these two domains was sheer coincidence. Coincidence... too many coincidences...

t_ch: 你讨厌巧合吗？
t_en: Do you hate coincidences?

t_ch: 现象与本质，火光和影子。如果巧合的出现率趋向饱和，在「巧合」的背后，一定存在「必然」的本质。
t_en: Phenomenon and essence, sparks and shadows... If the rate of coincidences appearing approaches saturation, there must be an essential "inevitability" beneath such "coincidence."

t_ch: 帕提维娅接收到的未知信号就是一个巧合，但她因此成为了学派战争时期最伟大的学者。
t_en: The unknown signal that Patavia received was a coincidence, but that was how she became the greatest scholar during the Scholars' Strife.

t_ch: 「不可知域」…我已经迫不及待地想知道，还有什么在等着我们了。
t_en: The Unknowable Domain... I already can't wait to find out what awaits us.

t_ch: 虽然，自始至终，我们都没能解开「不可知域」形成的原因。但至少我们搞清楚了帕提维娅博士在「学派战争」时期的经历。
t_en: Though we never managed to clarify the cause of what formed the Unknowable Domain, we are at least clear on Doctor Patavia's experiences during the Scholars' Strife.

t_ch: 她最终利用「权杖」系统扩展思维，甚至差点实现了鲁珀特二世未能完成的<color=#dbc291ff>自我加冕</color>。
t_en: She eventually used the Scepter system to expand her intellect, even almost accomplishing the <color=#dbc291ff>self-coronation</color> that Rubert II could not complete.

t_ch: 她本可以成为「完美的学者」，拓宽宇宙的知识总量，但自我怀疑却最终让她止步了。
t_en: She could have become "the perfect scholar," broadening the overall quantity of knowledge in the universe, but self-doubt eventually made her halt her advance.

t_ch: 唉……她只能遗憾地为世人留下那道孤波谜题，却不能成为解谜的天才。
t_en: *sigh* ...She could only regrettably leave behind that solitary wave conundrum as her legacy, unable to become the genius who solved that dilemma.

t_ch: 提问：这个「不可知域」是一个不经意的巧合？如蝴蝶扇动翅膀。还是一个圈套？邀你我跌落其间。
t_en: Question: Is this Unknowable Domain an accidental coincidence? Like a butterfly flapping its wings? Or is it a trap, luring us into its abyss?

t_ch: 谁能为天才设置陷阱？在天才的研究里。
t_en: Who can set a trap for a genius within a genius' research.

t_ch: 答案：另一位天才。鲁珀特一世虽然被寂静领主杀死，但他的知识却借由二世的头脑重生。
t_en: Answer: Another genius. Rubert I was slain by the Lord of Silence, but his knowledge was reborn with the Second Emperor's brain.

t_ch: 同为俱乐部成员，我们都明白——任何一位天才都不容小觑，哪怕对方死去许久。
t_en: As Society members, we all understand that no genius is to be underestimated, no matter how long it has been since their demise.

t_ch: 与以往不同，黑塔并未反驳。
t_en: Herta does not retort — a departure from her usual self.

t_ch: 先做好最坏的打算吧……
t_en: Let's just prepare for the worst...

t_ch: 你感觉这阵沉默持续了很久，对天才而言，一秒的思考，或许恰如人们一个琥珀纪的思考那般深远。
t_en: For you, this momentary silence seems like an eternity — To a genius, however, a second's contemplation may just be as profound as the thoughts of an average person over an Amber Era.

t_ch: 与以往相同，我和斯蒂芬会在外部全力保障你们的安全。无论如何，请二位务必平安归来。
t_en: Just like before, Stephen and I will fully guarantee your safety outside. No matter what happens, you two must return safe and sound.

t_ch: 等我们的好消息吧。
t_en: Wait for our good news.

t_ch: 终于到解谜时刻了。
t_en: It's finally time to solve the puzzle.

t_ch: 爆帝皇金币，好耶！
t_en: Burst Emperor coins, awright!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，在接入模拟宇宙前，有件事需要提前告诉你。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, before connecting to the Simulated Universe, I have something to tell you.

t_ch: 如果发现情况不对，马上往回跑，千万不要回头。
t_en: If you sense something amiss, turn around and run back with all you've got. Do NOT look behind you.

t_ch: 「不可知域」已经彻底消散了。
t_en: The Unknowable Domain has completely dissipated.

t_ch: 按照黑塔女士的计划，原本打算将鲁珀特二世的数据体作为第五位天才邀至席间，未曾料想，竟是「寂静领主」的数据体活化。
t_en: Madam Herta's original plan was to turn Rubert II's data into the fifth genius and invite them to our table, but we did not expect the data entity emulating the Lord of Silence to gain sentience.

t_ch: 这只剧毒的蝴蝶从模拟宇宙中挣脱，不仅没有同我们合作的打算，反倒留下遍地狼藉。
t_en: This lethal butterfly escaped from the Simulated Universe. Not only was she disinterested in cooperating with us, she also left chaos in her wake.

t_ch: 唉～越想越可惜，她那操弄概率的能力，和模拟宇宙简直是天生的一对。
t_en: Ahhh... it's a terrible pity, the more I think about it. Her proficiency in manipulating probability is absolutely tailored to the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 针对这次的外部入侵事件，有必要对模拟宇宙的防火墙进行相应的迭代处理。
t_en: This external intrusion has raised the need to make the necessary improvements to the Simulated Universe's firewall.

t_ch: 最好预留一个小口，告诉她：「有空常来坐坐。」
t_en: Best to leave an inconspicuous backdoor to tell her: "Come back again when you have time."

t_ch: 出现的到底是真的波尔卡…？
t_en: Was it really Polka who appeared...?

t_ch: …还是假的波尔卡？
t_en: ...Or was it an imposter?

t_ch: 真假并没有那么重要。「寂静领主」似乎拥有沿着因果链条自由现身的力量。
t_en: Her true identity doesn't matter. The Lord of Silence appears to have the power to show up along the chains of karma.

t_ch: 模仿她的数据体也好，研究她的档案也罢……恐怕只是直呼名字，就能让她有所感应，切，快赶上会魔法的老妖婆了。算了，我们最好不要再谈这个话题了。
t_en: Whether it's a data entity emulating her or a document recording investigations about her... I fear that she will feel something as long as her name is called out. Bah, she's almost like an old hag who knows magic. Forget it. We should just drop this topic.

t_ch: 提问：既然波尔卡女士不想加入模拟宇宙，她又为何要主动落入我们的陷阱？
t_en: Question: If Madam Polka does not wish to join the Simulated Universe, why would she intentionally walk into our trap?

t_ch: 好奇？或者单纯是为了好玩？要么就是报复我们在背后念叨她的年纪。
t_en: Curiosity? Or just out of plain old fun? Either that, or she's taking revenge on us discussing her age behind her back.

t_ch: 就这破俱乐部的人，有几个是精神正常的？
t_en: Is there anyone in this damned Society without a few screws loose?

t_ch: 再者说，最后差些落入陷阱的，反倒是为她布设罗网的我们。
t_en: Also, the hunter almost became the hunted. We almost got caught in a trap despite our own trap-laying.

t_ch: 那么，我们的第五位合作者……
t_en: Then, our fifth collaborator is...

t_ch: 还是看看，阮•梅能不能成功把余清涂拉进来吧。
t_en: We'd better see if Ruan Mei can successfully drag Yu Qingtu into this.

t_ch: 很高兴能看到你们安全地回来，推论：这或许是此次课题最大的一项收获。
t_en: I am glad that you are all back safe and sound. Deduction: This could be the greatest information haul gained from this research topic.

t_ch: 模拟宇宙的损坏…
t_en: The damage on the Simulated Universe...

t_ch: 用有机生命的修辞来说，我们确实在模拟宇宙里「玩过头」了。
t_en: In the vernacular of organic lifeforms, we have definitely "overdone" it in the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 不过这并非无益，逻辑：两位宝贵的合作伙伴，其权重远高于个体安全和运行效率。
t_en: But it was not all in vain. Logic: The two research partners are of far more import than individual safety and operational efficacy.

t_ch: 斯蒂芬去哪儿了？
t_en: Where did Stephen go?

t_ch: 斯蒂芬表示，他在目睹了寂静领主的技术后「大受震撼」，想要做出一套与她相同的面部认知滤网。
t_en: Stephen expressed that his "mind was blown" after witnessing the Lord of Silence's methods, and wishes to make a facial recognition filter mesh similar to hers.

t_ch: 复述：「再也不用担心被人看见了。」
t_en: Reiteration: "No more worries about being seen by others again."

t_ch: 关于鲁珀特二世…
t_en: About Rubert II...

t_ch: 二世为自己打造的衣冠冢，还有墓前的花束。打破了我们一直以来，对反有机方程乃至帝皇的认知。
t_en: The cenotaph that the Second built for himself and the flowers by the tomb overturned what we thought we knew about the Anti-Organic Equation, or even about the Emperor.

t_ch: 曾有位朋友告诉我，「个体间存在的差异，远比有机和无机生命间的差异更大。」
t_en: A friend told me once: "The differences between individuals far surpass those between organic and inorganic lives."

t_ch: 跨越种族传播的反有机病毒，也佐证了这一观点。
t_en: The spread of the Anti-Organic Virus across the species barrier has also proven this perspective.

t_ch: 虽然二世身为有机体，与智械有所不同。但我愿相信，反有机方程之下，仍然存在无法被掩埋的「情感」。
t_en: Rubert II was an organic being, different from Intellitrons. But I choose to believe that, under the power of the Anti-Organic Equation, there still laid emotions that could not be buried.

t_ch: 以这一发现作为前提，结论：编写反转反有机方程的程序，其可行性并不为0。
t_en: With this discovery as the premise... Conclusion: The probability of composing a program that reverses the Anti-Organic Equation program is greater than 0.

t_ch: 虽然寂静领主破坏了模拟宇宙复现的「权杖」系统，但关于下个阶段的研究，她的话倒给了我不错的灵感……
t_en: Though the Lord of Silence has destroyed the Scepter system reproduced by the Simulated Universe, her words gave me plenty of inspiration for the research's next stage...

t_ch: 看你忧心忡忡的，还有什么要说的吗？我现在心情不太好，只给你一次提问的机会，想清楚了再问。
t_en: From your anxiety-laden expression, I take it you have more to say? I'm not in a particularly good mood right now, so you only get one question. Think hard before asking it.

t_ch: 孤波算法难题的解…
t_en: The solitary waves theory's solut—

t_ch: 啧，偏偏问了最麻烦的问题……
t_en: Tsk, you just had to ask the hardest question...

t_ch: 解开孤波算法难题时，我得到了一个常数。
t_en: When I solved the solitary waves theory, I obtained a constant.

t_ch: 当宇宙的不稳定指数超过它，未来便会倾向混沌的未知，被迷雾包裹。当不稳定指数低于它，未来则会落入确定的轨迹，再无自由意志。
t_en: When the universe's unstable index exceeds it, the future will veer towards the chaotic unknown, enveloped in fog. When the unstable index is less than it, the future will fall into an ascertained trajectory, and free will ceases to exist.

t_ch: 但是，在这个疯癫的宇宙，就算是常数也会坍塌和变化。「智识」、「神秘」…各个命途的力量相互挤压，不断改写常数的定值。
t_en: But even constants collapse and change in this universe of madness. Erudition, Enigmata... The various Path powers press against one another, continually rewriting the constant.
